items.description: Long horizontal pen stroke with curved, hooked or leaf-like terminal flourish on the left end, extending beneath the final line of text.
items.scholarly_note: Decorative pen trial or tailpiece; ink matches main text suggesting execution by the scribe or a near-contemporary hand at the close of the text block.

items.description: Three large, bold calligraphic symbols resembling ornate capital or abstract letter forms (C-like and B-like shapes with internal curves and lobes) drawn in dark ink immediately right of the flourish.
items.scholarly_note: Pen trials of enlarged letter forms or decorative motifs; scale and execution differ from the main Gothic book hand, possibly practice strokes or embellishment.

items.description: Inscription 'o dake.' written in smaller, slightly irregular script to the right of the three symbols.
items.scholarly_note: Later pen trial or casual annotation; letter forms and module differ from the primary 13th-century scribe, likely a post-medieval reader or owner's mark.

items.description: Small numerals 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 placed at regular intervals at the ends of lines along the right edge of the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Post-medieval editorial line or stanza numbering added for reference during scholarly study or facsimile production (18th–20th century).

items.description: Numeral '14' visible in the upper left corner above the text block.
items.scholarly_note: Folio or page designation from an earlier or alternative foliation system; does not correspond to the current processing label of page 23.

items.description: Faint linear marks or possible offset letters ('nota'-like) visible on the left side of the page in the raw scan, approximately mid-height.
items.scholarly_note: Possible faint marginal annotation, offset ink from an adjacent leaf, or later reference mark; cleaned versions obscure it and confirmation requires advanced imaging.

items.description: Brownish age discoloration and staining band along the top edge, darker at the upper right corner.
items.scholarly_note: Typical vellum aging, handling stains, and possible water or binding contact damage; consistent with calfskin preparation of the period.

items.description: Darkened, worn, and irregularly stained right edge with rounding at the corners.
items.scholarly_note: Cumulative edge wear from use and binding; iron-gall ink may exacerbate localized degradation over centuries.

items.description: Enlarged opening 'P' with extended tail at the start of the text; minor variations in ink density and occasional thick strokes within the body text.
items.scholarly_note: Standard Gothic book-hand section initial; minor density variations likely functional (pen loading) rather than deliberate corrections.

scratches: Fine surface abrasions and hair-side texture visible across margins and blank areas; no deep incised lines.
Minor horizontal laid-line texture from parchment preparation visible under raking light; does not affect legibility.
damage_notes: Edge wear and corner rounding, heaviest at top-right and bottom-right.
Perimeter browning, cockling, and age spots typical of 13th-century Icelandic vellum after 750+ years.
No holes, perforations, or significant losses within the text block or margins.
Ink remains largely dark and legible with only localized minor fading; no heavy offset or ghosting visible on this side.
summary: Page 23 of Codex Regius displays a clean, well-written block of Eddic verse in Gothic book hand with minimal original scribal intervention. The principal non-text features are confined to the lower margin: a decorative horizontal pen flourish with hooked terminal, three large calligraphic pen trials, and the later inscription 'o dake.' Small post-medieval line numbers (5–25) run along the right edge and an older '14' appears at top left. The parchment shows characteristic age-related edge staining, wear, and surface texture but no major damage, holes, figurative doodles, animals, faces, or clear corrections inside the text block; the script is fluent and uniform.
